title: Kaitiakitanga in Action: Guardians of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream | Te Mahi Kaitiaki mō Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
subject: English
culturalContext: Students will explore kaitiakitanga through the lens of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, a significant waterway in Ngāti Kahungunu rohe. The stream's history as a traditional food source and transport route, its current ecological challenges, and community restoration efforts will form the cultural foundation for developing persuasive communication skills.
learningObjectives: Analyze and evaluate persuasive language techniques in texts advocating for environmental kaitiakitanga
Create a persuasive speech that incorporates Ngāti Kahungunu perspectives on water guardianship
Collaboratively develop and present a multimedia campaign proposal for stream restoration
Apply tikanga Māori concepts of whanaungatanga and manaakitanga in community engagement

activities.title: He Kōrero mō Te Awa | The Story of Our Stream
activities.description: Students explore historical photographs, maps, and oral histories of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku. They analyze how language has been used to describe the stream across different time periods and cultural perspectives, identifying persuasive techniques and cultural values embedded in the texts.

activities.title: Te Reo Kairangi | Language of Persuasion
activities.description: Students examine contemporary texts advocating for environmental protection (local council documents, iwi submissions, youth climate speeches). Through guided analysis, they identify and practice using rhetorical devices, emotional appeals, and evidence-based arguments specific to environmental advocacy.

activities.title: Hui with Kaitiaki | Learning from Guardians
activities.description: Students prepare questions and interview local kaitiaki from Ngāti Kahungunu environmental teams and regional council representatives. They learn firsthand about current restoration efforts and the language used to advocate for the stream in different contexts (marae, council meetings, community events).

activities.title: He Korero Whakamanawa | Crafting Our Speeches
activities.description: Using templates and models, students draft 3-minute persuasive speeches advocating for specific actions to protect Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku. They incorporate cultural references, local data, and persuasive techniques learned, practicing delivery with peer feedback focused on both content and presentation skills.

activities.title: Te Whakatairanga | Campaign Presentation
activities.description: Groups develop and present a multimedia campaign proposal to an authentic audience (local kaitiaki, community board representative). Proposals must include written rationale, visual elements, and specific language strategies for different community stakeholders, demonstrating understanding of targeted persuasion.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu Iwi Incorporated environmental reports and policy documents
Hawke's Bay Regional Council 'State of the Environment' reports specific to Mangakōtukutuku catchment
Local kaitiaki and environmental officers from Manaaki Whenua - Landcare Research
Digital archive of historical Hawke's Bay Tribune articles about the stream through Papers Past

tasks: Written persuasive speech with rhetorical analysis self-reflection
Multimedia campaign proposal document with targeted language strategies
Group presentation to community stakeholders with Q&A response

nzcAlignment: English: Processes and Strategies - Integrating sources of information, processes, and strategies to identify, form, and express ideas
English: Ideas - Show understanding of ideas within, across, and beyond texts
English: Language Features - Use a range of language features appropriately for effect
Social Sciences: Place and Environment - Understand how people interact with natural and cultural environments
